Building India's Future, One Meal at a Time

Building compassionate food systems.

Bodhi Greens Foundation works at the intersection of child nutrition, compassion education, and food systems — in India's classrooms, kitchens, and gardens.

Our Mission

"The child who learns what good food is will feed their family differently. The family that eats differently changes what the farmer grows. The farmer who grows differently heals the land."

We are building a long-term institution for compassionate food systems. Children are the entry point. Compassion is the destination. Food is the medium through which we travel between them.

Entry Point
The Child
The Medium
Food & Nutrition
The Destination
Compassionate Systems
Our full story →

The problem we refuse to accept

India's nutrition crisis is not
what you think it is.

The problem is not a shortage of food. It is a crisis of knowledge — what children learn, what families believe, and what systems reward.

0%
of Indian children under 5 are stunted due to chronic undernutrition
NFHS-5, Government of India, 2021
0%
of childhood anaemia in India is linked to inadequate dietary diversity
UNICEF State of the World's Children, 2022
1 in 3
Indian adults will face a diet-related chronic disease by 2035
ICMR-NIN Dietary Guidelines, 2024

"These statistics are not about food. They are about knowledge — and that is a problem that education can solve."

What We Do

Three programmes. One integrated system.

Each programme feeds the others — the trained teacher brings the garden alive; the garden feeds the meal; the meal changes the child; the child changes the family.

Flagship
🎓

School Nutrition Initiative

Nutrition literacy woven into the curriculum. Structured sessions taught by our trained educators, reaching 12,000+ children across 6 states.

Learn more
Expanding
🌱

School Garden Programme

Children who grow food trust food. Living vegetable gardens that connect children to the source of their nutrition — and feed directly into midday meals.

Learn more
Core
👩‍🏫

Teacher Training Academy

One teacher reaches 40 children and 80 families. Our intensive programme creates nutrition-literate educators with a lasting multiplier effect.

Learn more
👩‍🏫

Teacher Training

Structured capacity building for educators in whole food nutrition science, classroom facilitation, and behaviour observation.

🧒

Student Activities

Age-appropriate, hands-on nutrition sessions connecting food to health, culture, environment, and compassion.

👨‍👩‍👧

Parent Engagement

Community sessions connecting school learning to the home, supporting families in informed food choices.

📦

School Toolkits & Nutrition Cards

Designed classroom resources covering food groups, plant-based nutrition, seasonal eating, and indigenous crops.

📋

Monitoring & Evaluation

Systematic monitoring of dietary habits, knowledge change, and programme adherence — evidence at every stage.

🤲

Volunteer Training

Building trained nutrition volunteers who extend the programme's reach into communities beyond school walls.

Building healthier communities through education, action, and partnership.

"We are not designing a nutrition programme. We are designing a system — where every child who learns to think about food becomes a thread in a larger web of change."

— Bodhi Greens Foundation

Life in Our Schools

Midday meal distribution, government school
Garden learning session
District-level teacher training workshop
Harvest day at the school garden
Hands-on vegetable identification class
India's Nutrition Crisis

Why We Exist

A child's lunch should never be their only meal.

"135 million Indian children suffer from chronic malnutrition. The solution is already in their schools. We simply need the will — and the funding — to act."

India's schools reach more than 250 million children every day. That is the most powerful nutrition delivery system the world has ever seen — and it is profoundly underutilised.

We believe every school can become a centre of nutritional intelligence, not just caloric delivery. We are building the systems, training the teachers, and growing the gardens that make this possible.

See our evidence →

Voices from the Field

The change we see in their eyes.

"Before the training, I taught nutrition as a chapter to be completed. Now I understand it is a way of seeing the world. My students ask different questions. They go home and ask their mothers different things."

Sunita Devi
Government Primary School Teacher, Rajasthan

"My daughter came home and told me tomatoes have Vitamin C. She insisted we add them to our dal every day. Now we do. That child has changed our kitchen."

Rekha Kumari
Parent, Government School, Bihar

"We planted spinach in our school garden. When we cooked it in the midday meal, the children ate more than usual — because they grew it themselves."

Meenakshi Rao
Headmistress, Zila Parishad School, Karnataka

Partner with Us

Your support changes what a child eats for the rest of their life.

Foundations, CSR partners, and individual donors — every rupee goes directly into classrooms, teacher training, and school gardens.

🏛️
Foundation Grants
Multi-year grants for teacher training academies, school garden pilots, and curriculum development. FCRA in progress.

🏢

NGO partnerships, government co-implementation, academic research, and pro-bono technical support.
